speaker: EDGAR
text: Yet better thus, and known to be contemned,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Than still contemned and flattered. To be worst,

speaker: EDGAR
text: The lowest and most dejected thing of Fortune,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Stands still in esperance, lives not in fear.

speaker: EDGAR
text: The lamentable change is from the best;

speaker: EDGAR
text: The worst returns to laughter. Welcome, then,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Thou unsubstantial air that I embrace.

speaker: EDGAR
text: The wretch that thou hast blown unto the worst

speaker: EDGAR
text: Owes nothing to thy blasts. But who comes here?

speaker: EDGAR
text: My father, poorly led? World, world, O world,

speaker: EDGAR
text: But that thy strange mutations make us hate thee,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Life would not yield to age.

speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: O my good lord, I have been your tenant

speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: And your father’s tenant these fourscore years.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Away, get thee away. Good friend, begone.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Thy comforts can do me no good at all;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Thee they may hurt.

speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: You cannot see your way.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I have no way and therefore want no eyes.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I stumbled when I saw. Full oft ’tis seen

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Our means secure us, and our mere defects

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Prove our commodities. O dear son Edgar,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: The food of thy abusèd father’s wrath,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Might I but live to see thee in my touch,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I’d say I had eyes again.

speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: How now? Who’s there?

speaker: EDGAR
text: O gods, who is ’t can say “I am at the worst”?

speaker: EDGAR
text: I am worse than e’er I was.

speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: ’Tis poor mad Tom.

speaker: EDGAR
text: And worse I may be yet. The worst is not

speaker: EDGAR
text: So long as we can say “This is the worst.”

speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: Fellow, where goest?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Is it a beggar-man?

speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: Madman and beggar too.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: He has some reason, else he could not beg.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I’ th’ last night’s storm, I such a fellow saw,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Which made me think a man a worm. My son

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Came then into my mind, and yet my mind

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Was then scarce friends with him. I have heard more since.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: As flies to wanton boys are we to th’ gods;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: They kill us for their sport.

speaker: EDGAR
text: How should this be?

speaker: EDGAR
text: Bad is the trade that must play fool to sorrow,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Ang’ring itself and others.—Bless thee, master.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Is that the naked fellow?

speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: Ay, my lord.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Then, prithee, get thee away. If for my sake

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Thou wilt o’ertake us hence a mile or twain

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I’ th’ way toward Dover, do it for ancient love,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And bring some covering for this naked soul,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Which I’ll entreat to lead me.

speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: Alack, sir, he is mad.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: ’Tis the time’s plague when madmen lead the blind.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Do as I bid thee, or rather do thy pleasure.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Above the rest, begone.

speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: I’ll bring him the best ’parel that I have,

speaker:
OLD
               
              MAN
text: Come on ’t what will.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Sirrah, naked fellow—

speaker: EDGAR
text:
Poor Tom’s a-cold.
                 
                Aside
                .
               I cannot daub it further.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Come hither, fellow.

speaker: EDGAR
text: And yet I must.—Bless thy sweet eyes, they bleed.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Know’st thou the way to Dover?

speaker: EDGAR
text: Both stile and gate, horseway and footpath.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Poor Tom hath been scared out of his good wits.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Bless thee, good man’s son, from the foul fiend.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Five fiends have been in Poor Tom at once: of lust,

speaker: EDGAR
text: as Obidicut; Hobbididance, prince of dumbness;

speaker: EDGAR
text: Mahu, of stealing; Modo, of murder; Flibbertigibbet,

speaker: EDGAR
text: of mopping and mowing, who since possesses

speaker: EDGAR
text: chambermaids and waiting women. So, bless

speaker: EDGAR
text: thee, master.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Here, take this purse, thou whom the heavens’ plagues

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Have humbled to all strokes. That I am wretched

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Makes thee the happier. Heavens, deal so still:

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Let the superfluous and lust-dieted man,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: That slaves your ordinance, that will not see

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Because he does not feel, feel your power quickly.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: So distribution should undo excess

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And each man have enough. Dost thou know Dover?

speaker: EDGAR
text: Ay, master.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: There is a cliff, whose high and bending head

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Looks fearfully in the confinèd deep.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Bring me but to the very brim of it,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And I’ll repair the misery thou dost bear

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: With something rich about me. From that place

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I shall no leading need.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Give me thy arm.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Poor Tom shall lead thee.
